prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.406) and high seasonal capture variance (0.6466), with THETA leading in seasonality while ONLY_FT_rf dominates trend tracking and overall RMSE. The varying true trend directions across folds (up/down/up) combined with early/late horizon specialists differing supports a DBA combination approach that can leverage complementary model strengths rather than relying on single-model dominance.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_FT_rf", "late": "NaiveMovingAverage"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ETS", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_svr", "THETA", "CWT_svr", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.406,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.406) and high seasonal capture variance (0.6466), with THETA leading in seasonality while ONLY_FT_rf dominates trend tracking and overall RMSE. The varying true trend directions across folds (up/down/up) combined with early/late horizon specialists differing supports a DBA combination approach that can leverage complementary model strengths rather than relying on single-model dominance."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35, "top_k": 3}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.323 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.406 favor robust combination approaches like dba_combination for handling phase shifts. n_windows=3 is very low, so small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seas_corr_variance=0.6466 and varying trend directions (up/down/up). Score preset robust_smape selected because avg SMAPE=0.901 > 0.3 (scale-sensitive series)."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with types: selection(2), baseline(1), weighted(1) - good diversity. No leakage detected (dba_combination is baseline, others use anti-leakage selection). From universe leaderboard, ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 beats current best dba_combination on all metrics (RMSE 18.21 vs 18.93, SMAPE 0.834 vs 0.855, MAPE 0.503 vs 0.517). Adding this stacking candidate improves performance diversity and adds a new type to the set.", "changes": ["Added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 from universe leaderboard - it has better aggregate scores than current best_per_horizon_by_validation"], "when_good": ""}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.10"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Add inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.10 which is a top performer in universe leaderboards (best POCID=66.67). Current set already has good diversity with selection, weighted, baseline, and stacking methods. With n_windows=3, keeping candidate count at 6 avoids overfitting while improving POCID coverage.", "changes": ["added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.10 for better POCID coverage"], "when_good": "When n_windows increases to 6+, consider adding ridge_stacking variants with different l2 values"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.406) and high seasonal capture variance (0.6466), with THETA leading in seasonality while ONLY_FT_rf dominates trend tracking and overall RMSE. The varying true trend directions across folds (up/down/up) combined with early/late horizon specialists differing supports a DBA combination approach that can leverage complementary model strengths rather than relying on single-model dominance.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.323 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.406 favor robust combination approaches like dba_combination for handling phase shifts. n_windows=3 is very low, so small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seas_corr_variance=0.6466 and varying trend directions (up/down/up). Score preset robust_smape selected because avg SMAPE=0.901 > 0.3 (scale-sensitive series).

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with types: selection(2), baseline(1), weighted(1) - good diversity. No leakage detected (dba_combination is baseline, others use anti-leakage selection). From universe leaderboard, ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 beats current best dba_combination on all metrics (RMSE 18.21 vs 18.93, SMAPE 0.834 vs 0.855, MAPE 0.503 vs 0.517). Adding this stacking candidate improves performance diversity and adds a new type to the set.
changes: Added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 from universe leaderboard - it has better aggregate scores than current best_per_horizon_by_validation

statistician.rationale: Add inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.10 which is a top performer in universe leaderboards (best POCID=66.67). Current set already has good diversity with selection, weighted, baseline, and stacking methods. With n_windows=3, keeping candidate count at 6 avoids overfitting while improving POCID coverage.
changes: added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.10 for better POCID coverage
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to 6+, consider adding ridge_stacking variants with different l2 values
